info.name: Cybership — UPS Rating API
info.description:
Postman collection for the UPS Rating API endpoints wrapped by the Cybership Carrier Integration Service.

This collection documents the raw UPS API calls that the service makes under the hood. Use it to:
- Understand the exact request/response shapes
- Test against the UPS sandbox with real credentials
- Debug integration issues by replaying requests manually

## Setup
1. Set `ups_client_id` and `ups_client_secret` in collection variables
2. Optionally set `ups_account_number`
3. Run the **OAuth Token** request first — it auto-saves the Bearer token
4. Run any Rate request

## Environments
- **Sandbox**: `https://wwwcie.ups.com` (default)
- **Production**: `https://onlinetools.ups.com`

variable.description: UPS API base URL. Sandbox: https://wwwcie.ups.com | Production: https://onlinetools.ups.com
UPS shipper account number (optional, for negotiated rates)
Auto-populated by the OAuth Token request

item.name: Authentication
item.description: UPS OAuth 2.0 client-credentials flow. Run this first to obtain a Bearer token for subsequent API calls.

exec: pm.test('Token saved to collection variables', function () {
pm.test('Token request failed with status ' + pm.response.code, function () {

request.description:
Acquire an OAuth 2.0 access token using the client-credentials grant type.

The token is valid for approximately 4 hours (14399 seconds). The test script automatically saves the token to the `access_token` collection variable for use by subsequent requests.

**Our service handles this transparently** via `UpsAuthManager` — token caching, concurrent deduplication, and auto-refresh on expiry.

item.name: Rating
item.description:
UPS Rating API endpoints. These are the raw API calls that `UpsRatingOperation` makes.

- **Shop** (`/Shop`) — returns quotes for ALL available services
- **Rate** (`/Rate`) — returns a quote for a SPECIFIC service

exec: pm.test('Each shipment has service code and total charges', function () {

request.description:
**Rate Shopping** — returns quotes for all available UPS services for the given shipment.

This is the endpoint used when `serviceLevel` is omitted from the `DomainRateRequest`. The service sends to `/Shop` and receives multiple `RatedShipment` entries.

**Service maps this to:** `UpsRateMapper.resolveRequestOption()` → `"Shop"`

**Note:** The `Service` field is intentionally omitted from the Shipment — this tells UPS to return all available services.

### UPS Service Codes
| Code | Service | ServiceLevel |
|------|---------|-------------|
| 01 | UPS Next Day Air | Overnight |
| 02 | UPS 2nd Day Air | TwoDay |
| 03 | UPS Ground | Ground |
| 12 | UPS 3 Day Select | ThreeDay |
| 13 | UPS Next Day Air Saver | Express |
| 14 | UPS Next Day Air Early | Overnight |
| 59 | UPS 2nd Day Air A.M. | TwoDay |

response.name: 200 — Multiple Service Quotes

exec: pm.test('Service code matches requested service', function () {

request.description:
**Rate for a specific service** — returns a quote for UPS Ground only.

This is the endpoint used when `serviceLevel` is provided in the `DomainRateRequest`. The service sends to `/Rate` with the `Service` block included in the Shipment.

**Service maps this to:** `UpsRateMapper.resolveRequestOption()` → `"Rate"`

**Key difference from Shop:** The `Service` field is present with code `"03"` (Ground). UPS returns a single `RatedShipment` object (not an array).

### Available Service Codes
| Code | Description |
|------|------------|
| 01 | UPS Next Day Air |
| 02 | UPS 2nd Day Air |
| 03 | UPS Ground |
| 12 | UPS 3 Day Select |
| 13 | UPS Next Day Air Saver |
| 14 | UPS Next Day Air Early |
| 59 | UPS 2nd Day Air A.M. |

**Multi-package shipment** — demonstrates rating with 2 packages and multi-line addresses.

Key points:
- `AddressLine` is an array — our mapper converts `addressLine1` + `addressLine2` into this array
- `Package` is an array — our mapper maps `packages[]` from the domain request
- Uses `"02"` (Customer Supplied) as the default packaging type

**Negotiated Rates** — requests UPS account-specific discounted rates.

Requires a valid `ShipperNumber` (UPS account number). The response will include a `NegotiatedRateCharges` block alongside the standard `TotalCharges`.

**Note:** Our service's type definitions support this field (`ShipmentRatingOptions.NegotiatedRatesIndicator`) but the mapper does not currently set it. This is documented as a future improvement.
